name: Long-context evaluation (NoLiMa, NeedleChain, Haystack-style)
context_primitives: Synthetic or semi-synthetic long prompts testing whether models retrieve or reason over distant content. NoLiMa reduces lexical overlap between needle and query. NeedleChain stresses settings where the entire context is query-relevant. Haystack-style work evaluates heterogeneous, noisy, retrieval-biased long contexts closer to agents.
deployment_pattern: Researchers publish code + arXiv; practitioners use findings to sanity-check vendor 'infinite context' claims and to choose RAG vs. long-window strategies.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: NoLiMa reports sharp accuracy drops for some models as length grows when overlap is controlled. NeedleChain argues classic NIAH can overestimate capability. Haystack-style benchmarks highlight failures under realistic noise and tool-like retrieval.
ecosystem_integrations: Feeds into model cards, procurement diligence, and internal eval suites for enterprise agents.
differentiators: Splits 'find a secret string' from 'reason over everything' from 'survive agentic mess'—distinct failure modes.
significance_for_builders: Do not rely on a single NIAH demo; run task-specific evals (code + logs + tools) and assume retrieval quality matters as much as raw window size.
key_timeline_or_milestones: NoLiMa 2025; NeedleChain 2025; Haystack Engineering arXiv 2510.07414 (agentic long-context).

primary_sources.title: NoLiMa — Long-Context Evaluation Beyond Literal Matching
